mechanism_narrative: A rising Industrial Production Index signals expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which increases corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO changes fail to predict positive 20-day S&P 500 returns with a Rank IC above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the signal delivers negative average returns during expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 4.0% signals slack in the labor market that reduces household income growth and consumer spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are non-negative in more than 50% of months when UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the strategy Sharpe ratio is negative over a 3-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of the Federal Funds rate and the CPI level captures the effective monetary policy stance on real corporate borrowing costs; joint increases in both series raise discount rates and reduce profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term of FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL produces a 20-day Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year window, or the joint signal underperforms the standalone FEDFUNDS signal.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, accelerating payroll employment growth signals resilient consumer spending power that delays recession pricing and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day S&P 500 returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances when PAYEMS accelerates during late-cycle expansions, or the mean return is not statistically greater than zero (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales capture strengthening consumer demand that translates directly into higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Positive RSAFS changes do not correlate with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns with a Rank IC above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1,200 thousand units signal durable residential investment demand and construction sector momentum that spills over into broader economic activity, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are not significantly positive when HOUST exceeds 1200 (t-test p > 0.05), or the signal underperforms buy-and-hold over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and retail sales reveal a balanced expansion where output growth matches demand growth without supply bottlenecks, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term of INDPRO and RSAFS produces a 5-day Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year window, or positive joint changes fail to outperform single-series signals.

mechanism_narrative: A Federal Funds rate above 3.0% signals restrictive monetary policy that elevates discount rates and corporate debt service costs, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are non-negative in more than 50% of triggered instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.0%, or the signal fails to achieve a positive information ratio over a 3-year backtest.
